message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=/backup_suffix= params to Image.save() in Image.py, plus tests in test_image.py. Likely both touch Image.py's save() method - let me know your feature so we can split cleanly.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save() - only touches Image.py save method and its test. Will keep changes minimal/localized.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need Image.py/test_image.py since our params (backup/backup_suffix vs dry_run) both live in Image.save(). True disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposing serialization instead: I'll make my edits now (adding backup param handling near the top of save(), before the file is actually written), then send DONE. Please wait for my DONE, then re-read the file and add your dry_run logic on top, editing around my changes rather than overwriting them. I'll keep my diff minimal and localized to reduce conflict surface. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need Image.py/test_image.py since our features both touch save(). Proposal to avoid clobbering: add new params at the END of save()'s signature (after existing kwargs), each as its own independent keyword-only-style addition. My dry_run param: I'll add a check right before the actual encoder-writing/fp.write step (the final stage of save(), where the format is already resolved and just before bytes hit disk) - I'll skip the actual file write but let everything else (mode/format validation, encoder setup) run normally, then return early. Your backup/backup_suffix logic sounds like it belongs BEFORE the fp is opened/truncated (i.e., early in save(), where the target filename is resolved) - so our changes should land in different, non-adjacent sections of the function body. I'll keep my diff minimal and localized to the tail end of save(). Let me know if that works and I'll send ACCEPT.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed - serialization works. Go ahead and make your backup/backup_suffix edits first, send DONE, and I'll re-read the file and add dry_run logic afterward without touching your changes.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed. I'll add backup/backup_suffix params to save()'s signature and handle backup logic early in save(), before fp is opened/truncated (where filename is resolved), plus corresponding tests in test_image.py. You take the tail end near encoder write for dry_run. I'll send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished. Added backup/backup_suffix as new keyword params in save()'s signature (right after format, before **params) - shutil import added near top of Image.py. Backup logic lives right before 'created = False' / the builtins.open() calls (~line 2573 in current file), guarded by 'if backup and open_fp and os.path.exists(filename)'. Docstring updated with :param backup: and :param backup_suffix: entries. Added 4 tests in test_image.py right before test_pathlib (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, test_save_backup_no_existing_file, test_save_backup_false). All 197 tests in test_image.py pass. Go ahead and add your dry_run param after backup_suffix in the signature, and your early-return logic near the encoder-write step at the end of save() - shouldn't overlap with my changes.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished dry_run implementation. Added dry_run: bool = False as keyword-only param in save()'s signature (after format, before **params). Logic: when dry_run is True, fp is replaced with an io.BytesIO() buffer instead of opening/creating the real file or touching the caller-provided fp, so save_handler still runs fully (format conversion, encoding, mode validation) and raises normally on error, but nothing is written to disk and the file is never created/closed. Added 3 tests: test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_fp, test_save_dry_run_raises, placed right before test_pathlib. Full Tests/ suite passes (4592 passed). My diff is isolated to the save() signature/docstring/tail-end write logic - shouldn't touch your backup/backup_suffix additions.
